Phase 1 Design start. Mode A, T1, Python adapter pilot. Read run-manifest, intake-summary, tprd, mode, active-packages, toolchain, Go reference impl. RULE 0 propagated to all sub-agents via design/context/design-lead-brief.md.

rationale: Per agent definition: designer/interface/algorithm/concurrency/pattern-advisor/sdk-perf-architect run in parallel. T1 tier requires perf-architect. Mode A — no merge planner.
TPRD §15 Q1-Q6 already DECIDED by user/intake. Re-litigation would be tech-debt creation.
Preserves user's exception in __cause__; standard Python idiom; greppable in caller logs.

detail: PoolConfig, Pool (10 methods), AcquiredResource, PoolStats, PoolError + 4 descendants (PoolClosedError, PoolEmptyError, ConfigError, ResourceCreationError) — all have docstrings, traces-to markers, type hints

rationale: Simpler, fewer lines, mypy-strict compatible. Protocol+TypeVar variance is a known mypy footgun.
Single impl in pilot; structural ducks suffice. Future DistributedPool can extract Protocol post-hoc with zero break.

detail: All public signatures concrete (no Any); two type:ignore for sync/async hook union are documented in interfaces.md §4

rationale: Zero-alloc steady state push/pop; tighter cancel-rollback control; LIFO via pop() matches Python idiom.
TPRD Appendix B explicitly allows. Warm-cache reuse on most-recently-released resource. Default deque pop direction.
G107 requires complexity declaration backed by proof. Each operation step accounted; amortization argument bounded by concurrent-waiter count, not pool size.

detail: _acquire_with_timeout inner block, release inner block, _create_resource_via_hook (G109)

rationale: TPRD §3 Non-Goal: no thread pool. asyncio primitives self-enforce via RuntimeError on cross-loop access.
asyncio.CancelledError inherits from BaseException since 3.8. Catching only Exception would miss cancellation and leak the slot reservation.
Cross-language neutral name per language-agnostic decision board. T2-3 verdict for Phase B retrospective. Redundant alias outstanding_acquires for sanity-cross-validation.

detail: assert_no_leaked_tasks fixture asserts only on asyncio.all_tasks() snapshot. Reusable. T2-7 verdict.

rationale: frozen+slots on value types (Config, Stats); explicit __slots__ on behavior types (Pool, AcquiredResource); none on exceptions (Exception subclassing+slots is awkward).
Zero-dep + caller-controlled namespace. % formatting (deferred eval, level-filtered). Lazy import in error path.
Each is a Python idiom that COULD apply but adds complexity for no design benefit. Documenting absence to short-circuit overengineering-critic findings.

detail: snake_case methods, PascalCase classes, _private underscore, type hints on every public, frozen+slots on Config+Stats, sentinel error class hierarchy, # marker syntax — all confirmed pre-D2

rationale: Go bench launched at design time did not complete within wallclock cap. pool.go docstring states '10M+ ops/sec for cached resources' giving ~100ns per Get+Put cycle, ~50ns per Get. Documented recalibration path: impl phase re-measures and updates baselines/python/performance-baselines.json. If divergence >2x, perf-architect re-opens budget at H8.
10 minutes is the minimum-meaningful duration to detect drift in concurrency_units + heap_bytes via 30s polling (20 samples; minimum for trend fit). Backs G105.

detail: Pool.acquire happy/contention, Pool.try_acquire, Pool.aclose, scaling sweep — plus 3 bonus symbols (acquire_resource, release, stats) for completeness
No premature optimization in pilot v1; perf-exceptions.md is intentionally empty + documents future entry shape

G30-G38 + G108 are Go-package guardrails (NOT in active-packages.json for this Python pilot per §Guardrails-Manifest Notes block). Per CLAUDE.md rule 34, guardrails not in the active set are not run. D2 mechanical-check phase is a no-op for this run; design proceeds directly to D3 devil-review wave.

Empirical Go bench (go test -bench=. -benchmem -benchtime=2s) launched at design time did not complete within phase wallclock cap. Decision documented in perf-budget.md §0: declare oracle from pool.go doc-stated 10M ops/sec figure; impl phase re-measures and updates baselines/python/performance-baselines.json. If divergence >2x, perf-architect re-opens budget at H8. NOT tech debt — documented recalibration path.
every public method ≤4 user-facing params (PoolConfig + PoolStats synthesized __init__ at 5 are dataclass-exempt)

rationale: DD-001 (Pool 13 slots) and DD-002 (acquire ctx-mgr vs acquire_resource async) both have ACCEPT rationale. Quality 0.91; cross-language baseline check delta=-2pp within Lenient ±3pp band. D2 verdict: hold.

detail: hooks (caller-trust note SD-001), no deserialization, no PII, license-clean, no creds, no TOCTOU, no DoS
